summary: Resplit the former Popo ftPp_SpecialS object so that SpecialS ends at 0x80120E68 and new matching objects ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c cover the following text ranges. The build configuration moved ftPp_SpecialS.c from NonMatching to Matching and added matching entries for SpecialHi and SpecialLw. The PR also updated splits, local symbol names/scopes for data and sdata2 literals, and replaced broad/old ftPp_1211.h includes with move-specific headers. The bot report showed a net linked-code gain of +14416 bytes and 80 new matches, with many apparent broken matches caused by functions moving out of the old ftPp_SpecialS unit into the new split units.
what_changed: Changed the split for melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.c from text 0x8011F68C-0x80122EDC to 0x8011F68C-0x80120E68 and sdata2 0x804D9848-0x804D9898 to 0x804D9848-0x804D9860.

Added new split objects melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialHi.c at text 0x80120E68-0x80122898 with sdata2 0x804D9860-0x804D9890, and melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialLw.c at text 0x80122898-0x80122EDC with sdata2 0x804D9890-0x804D9898.
Updated configure.py so ftPp_SpecialS.c, ftPp_SpecialHi.c, and ftPp_SpecialLw.c are all built as Matching objects for Popo.
Moved a large body of code out of ftPp_SpecialS.c into new ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c source files; ftPp_SpecialS.c lost 1148 lines while SpecialHi gained 932 and SpecialLw gained 212.
Added or adjusted headers for ftPp_SpecialHi.h and ftPp_SpecialLw.h and removed the contents of ftPp_SpecialS.h in this slice.
Updated include sites in ftCo_0A01.c, ftNn_Init.c, and ftPp_Init.c to include ftPp_SpecialHi.h and ftPp_SpecialLw.h instead of the older ftPp_1211.h umbrella/placeholder header.
Converted several previously globally named data/sdata/sdata2 symbols such as ftPp_Init_804D9848 and ftPp_Unk0_804D3E10 to local @number symbols in symbols.txt.

The new ftPp_SpecialHi.c includes Popo/Nana coordination code, item/string interactions through itclimbersstring, ground/air SpecialHi enter/anim/IASA/phys/coll callbacks, and helper code visible in the diff excerpt.

The bot report recorded linked code increasing from the PR by +0.37%, +14416 bytes, with 80 new matches and 77 broken matches that mostly correspond to functions being moved from the old ftPp_SpecialS unit into the new SpecialHi/SpecialLw units.
smart_moves: The PR separated a monolithic Popo special-move file into move-specific compilation units, which made the object boundaries match the ROM split layout and allowed all three Popo special objects to be linked as Matching.
The split update was coordinated across splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, source files, and include dependencies rather than only moving code, reducing the chance of build/link inconsistencies.
Replacing ftPp_1211.h includes with ftPp_SpecialHi.h and ftPp_SpecialLw.h made dependencies more explicit and more searchable for later Popo/Nana work.

The symbol updates recognized that many float/string literals in the split region were local compiler artifacts and renamed them from misleading global ftPp_Init/ftPp_Unk0 names to local @number symbols.
The author left an explicit comment on the float-alignment workaround in review, making the questionable tactic visible for future cleanup instead of hiding it in the source movement.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching object contains multiple logical move ranges, resplitting can turn apparent per-function matches into linked object matches; expect bot reports to show many 'broken' old-unit matches paired with new matches in the new units.
A successful resplit needs address ranges for .text and .sdata2 to be cut consistently; here SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw use contiguous text ranges and non-overlapping sdata2 ranges.
After resplitting, revisit symbol scope: local literals and strings should not remain as global pseudo-symbols tied to unrelated init files.
Move-specific headers are preferable to placeholder or catch-all headers once a file is split and matched, because downstream files then document exactly which special-move interfaces they use.

For Ice Climbers work, Popo and Nana code may depend on each other: the SpecialHi code in this slice calls Player_GetEntityAtIndex for the partner and uses ftNn_Init helpers, so include and forward-declaration hygiene matters.

Bot match deltas after a resplit must be interpreted by unit migration, not only by raw broken-match counts; the PR had 77 broken matches in ftPp_SpecialS but 80 new matches in ftPp_SpecialHi/SpecialLw and a positive linked-code delta.
naming_conventions: Use move-specific Popo filenames and headers: ftPp_SpecialS.c, ftPp_SpecialHi.c, ftPp_SpecialLw.c and corresponding .h files.

Ground/air callback naming follows the observed pattern with Air prefix and callback suffixes: ftPp_SpecialHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Phys, ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll.
Local compiler-generated data and literal symbols in symbols.txt were represented as @number entries with scope:local rather than global ftPp_Init_804D98xx-style names.
The code keeps address-based placeholder function names when semantic names are not established, such as ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68 and ftPp_SpecialHi_8012280C.
Header cleanup should replace obsolete split placeholders such as ftPp_1211.h with the narrow special-move headers that declare the used functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used an explicit static sdata2_ordering function in ftPp_SpecialHi.c to force float/double literal ordering and alignment in .sdata2; the author called this a 'hideous hack' and asked if there was a better alignment method.
The sdata2_ordering helper mixes float and double constants, including 0.0f, 0.5, 3.0, 3.0f, 5.0f, 1.0f, -1.0f, and another 0.5, to influence literal layout.
Implemented a custom inline my_sqrtf using __frsqrte with repeated Newton-Raphson refinement and a volatile float output to match generated sqrt-like code in ftPp_SpecialS_80120E68.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in several functions in the excerpt to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used small static inline helpers such as checkNanaInRange and incrementMvAndCheck in the new SpecialHi code, apparently to preserve structure while matching callback code.
Preserved odd control flow where the original code returns without a value in ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0, including a labeled end block comment in the excerpt.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.comment: please forgive this hideous hack. If there's a better way to align the floats, I'm all ears!
review_feedback.lesson: The float/sdata2 alignment workaround was known to be ugly and should be revisited if the project develops a cleaner way to control literal ordering.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or replace the sdata2_ordering float-alignment hack in ftPp_SpecialHi.c.
Check whether ftPp_SpecialS.h being emptied/deleted was followed by complete header cleanup for all Popo special moves.
Search for subsequent renames of address-based functions in ftPp_SpecialHi.c and ftPp_SpecialLw.c to semantic names.
Look for other fighter resplit PRs that convert old umbrella headers like ftPp_1211.h into move-specific headers.
Audit whether the local @number symbol assignments for 0x804D9848-0x804D9898 stayed stable after later sdata2 work.
